title: spiraea callosa var. alba
intro: Spiraea callosa var. alba, also known as White Russian Spiraea, is a variety of flowering plant native to Siberia and Central Asia. It is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content, which are said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Spiraea callosa var. alba is typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
